rationale: The page contains a detailed table of international space missions (NASA, CNSA, ESA, ISRO, JAXA) and technical details about ISRO's CE20 Cryogenic Engine, providing high-quality factual content for question generation.
cleanedText:
4.9.2. OTHER AGENCIES
| Mission | Agency/Country | Key Details/Features |
|---|---|---|
| Lucy Mission | NASA | Launched in 2021, the name Lucy was chosen in honor of the fossilized human ancestor, named Lucy. Objective: First space mission to explore Jupiter Trojan asteroids (remnants from the early solar system). Trojan asteroids orbit in two "swarms" that lead and follow Jupiter. |
| Tianwen-2 probe | China National Space Administration (CNSA) | Objective: Collect samples from the asteroid 2016HO3 and explore the main-belt comet 311P. |
| Biomass Mission | European Space Agency (ESA) | Biomass is the first satellite to carry a P-band SAR. Objective: Map the world's forests to provide the first comprehensive measurements of forest biomass and stored carbon at a global scale. |
| Solar Orbiter | ESA & NASA | Objective: To take close-up images of the Sun, observe solar wind and the Sun's Polar Regions. Significance: Better understanding the solar cycle and magnetic field. |
| Chandrayaan-5 / LUPEX (Lunar Polar Exploration) Mission | ISRO & JAXA (Japan) | Objective: To study lunar volatile materials, of the South Pole. ISRO will primarily build the lander, while JAXA (Japan) will provide the rover. |
| Orbiting Carbon Observatory (OCO) | NASA | Objective: Global measurements of atmospheric carbon dioxide. |
| ESCAPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers) Mission | NASA | Objective: To investigate how the solar wind interacts with Mars' magnetic environment and how this interaction drives the planet's atmospheric escape. |
4.10. CRYOGENIC ENGINE
ISRO conducted the Boot-strap mode start test on CE20 Cryogenic Engine under vacuum conditions.
More on the News
• CE20 is India's largest cryogenic engine developed by Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre, Valiamala, Kerala.
• CE20 Cryogenic Engine powering the LVM3 upper stage, has been qualified for Gaganyaan missions.
Cryogenic Engine
• A Cryogenic engine uses both cryogenic fuel and oxidizer, liquefied at a very low temperature.
• Used in the last stage (or upper stage) of a rocket.
